LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DAVID DIPUMA'S MOTION TO AMEND THE COMPLAINT TO ADD LYFT, INC. AS A DEFENDANT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SAYS HE RECENTLY LEARNED IN DISCOVERY THAT DEFENDANT KARL DUFF WAS A LYFT DRIVER EN ROUTE TO PICK UP PASSENGERS WHEN A MOTOR VEHICLE ACCIDENT OCCURRED. DUFF ARGUES THAT THE SAME INFORMATION COULD HAVE BEEN LEARNED A YEAR AGO FROM A POLICE REPORT, BUT DOES NOT PROFFER A COPY OF THE REPORT. IN ANY EVENT, CALIFORNIA'S STRONG POLICY FAVORS LIBERAL AMENDMENT OF PLEADINGS. (MESSLER V. BRAGG MANAGEMENT CO. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 290, 296.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/RBU

Plaintiff David Dipuma'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Amended Complaint Adding Lyft, Inc. As A Defendant
Off Calendar Oct-05-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Plaintiff David Dipuma's motion to amend the complaint to add Lyft, Inc. as a defendant is GRANTED. Plaintiff says he recently learned in discovery that defendant Karl Duff was a Lyft driver en route to pick up passengers when a motor vehicle accident occurred. Duff argues that the same information could have been learned a year ago from a police report, but does not proffer a copy of the report. In any event, California's strong policy favors liberal amendment of pleadings. (Messler v. Bragg Management Co. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/rbu
